body_txt: In most mysteries, there will be suspects, and one of them will be the one pulling the strings. Could it be the rude curator Mr. Kim? The rival company CEO Mr. Jones? The creepy bellhop Mr. Smith? Or maybe it's the mysterious piano player Mr. Rodriguez? But wait. Notice how these suspects are all men. Aren't there any women around here ? Of course there are. But the protagonists either forgot to think about the possibility of them being the culprit, or they don't want to suspect them at all. In general, men commit the vast majority of violent crimes. However, thanks to the Double Standard that Females Are More Innocent , many assume that only men can commit crimes and will dismiss the idea of it being a woman. This is often Foreshadowing for The Reveal that Samus Is a Girl , the Tiny Tyrannical Girl , or the Lady Macbeth perpetrated the crime. At times, the detective figure will be shocked and may even refuse to believe it . This does not refer to any case where the perpetrator of a violent crime is a woman. It requires most or all of the characters to assume or take for granted that the perpetrator is a man , only for this assumption to be turned on its head. Female Monster Surprise is the explicitly supernatural or non-"realistic" version. Contrast Everyone Is a Suspect , where every male and female character is a suspect. Hackette is often a subtrope. The perception that women cannot commit sex crimes, as seen in Double Standard: Rape, Female on Male and Double Standard: Rape, Female on Female , is also common. As this trope pertains to a Plot Twist , this is a spoiler trope, and all spoilers will be unmarked .
